  1248→async def _verify_message_sent(page: Page, content: str, timeout: int = 5000) -> bool:
  1249→    """
  1250→    Verify that a message was actually sent by checking the conversation area.
  1251→
  1252→    Polls up to `timeout` ms with short intervals to handle async rendering.
  1253→
  1254→    Checks for:
  1255→    1. The sent message content appearing in the conversation
  1256→    2. The message input being cleared (indicating successful send)
  1257→    3. A sent/delivered indicator element
  1258→
  1259→    Args:
  1260→        page: Playwright page instance
  1261→        content: The message content that was sent
  1262→        timeout: Max time to wait for verification in ms
  1263→
  1264→    Returns:
  1265→        True if message send was verified
  1266→    """
  1267→    import time
  1268→
  1269→    # Short snippet for matching (first 20 chars, avoid template artifacts)
  1270→    snippet = content[:20].strip()
  1271→
  1272→    poll_interval_ms = 500
  1273→    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout / 1000.0
  1274→
  1275→    while True:
  1276→        try:
  1277→            # Strategy 1: Check if message content appears in conversation area
  1278→            verified = await page.evaluate('''(snippet) => {
  1279→                const chatSelectors = [
  1280→                    '[class*="message-list"]',
  1281→                    '[class*="chat-content"]',
  1282→                    '[class*="conversation"]',
  1283→                    '[class*="msg-list"]',
  1284→                    '[class*="im-message"]',
  1285→                    '[class*="chat-msg"]',
  1286→                ];
  1287→
  1288→                for (const sel of chatSelectors) {
  1289→                    const container = document.querySelector(sel);
  1290→                    if (container && container.textContent.includes(snippet)) {
  1291→                        return true;
  1292→                    }
  1293→                }
  1294→
  1295→                // Fallback: scan leaf-ish elements in the lower portion of the page
  1296→                const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;
  1297→                const elements = document.querySelectorAll('div, p, span');
  1298→                for (const el of elements) {
  1299→                    const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
  1300→                    if (rect.top > viewportHeight * 0.3 && el.textContent.includes(snippet)) {
  1301→                        if (el.textContent.length < snippet.length + 200) {
  1302→                            return true;
  1303→                        }
  1304→                    }
  1305→                }
  1306→
  1307→                return false;
  1308→            }''', snippet)
  1309→
  1310→            if verified:
  1311→                logger.debug("Message send verified: content found in conversation")
  1312→                return True
  1313→
  1314→            # Strategy 2: Check if input was cleared (indicates successful send)
  1315→            input_cleared = await page.evaluate('''() => {
  1316→                const inputs = document.querySelectorAll(
  1317→                    'textarea, [contenteditable="true"], input[type="text"]'
  1318→                );
  1319→                for (const input of inputs) {
  1320→                    const rect = input.getBoundingClientRect();
  1321→                    if (rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0) {
  1322→                        const value = input.value || input.textContent || '';
  1323→                        if (value.trim() === '') {
  1324→                            return true;
  1325→                        }
  1326→                    }
  1327→                }
  1328→                return false;
  1329→            }''')
  1330→
  1331→            if input_cleared:
  1332→                logger.debug("Message send verified: input field was cleared")
  1333→                return True
  1334→
  1335→            # Strategy 3: Check for sent/delivered indicators
  1336→            sent_indicators = [
  1337→                '[class*="sent"]',
  1338→                '[class*="delivered"]',
  1339→                '[class*="success"]',
  1340→                '[class*="msg-status"]',
  1341→            ]
  1342→            for selector in sent_indicators:
  1343→                try:
  1344→                    indicator = await page.query_selector(selector)
  1345→                    if indicator and await indicator.is_visible():
  1346→                        logger.debug(f"Message send verified: indicator found ({selector})")
  1347→                        return True
  1348→                except Exception:
  1349→                    continue
  1350→
  1351→        except Exception as e:
  1352→            logger.warning(f"Message verification poll failed: {e}")
  1353→
  1354→        # Check if we've exceeded the deadline
  1355→        if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
  1356→            break
  1357→
  1358→        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval_ms / 1000.0)
  1359→
  1360→    logger.warning("Could not verify message was sent after polling")
  1361→    return False
  1362→
  1363→
  1364→async def _check_existing_conversation(page: Page, maimai_id: str) -> bool:
  1365→    """
  1366→    Check if there's already an existing conversation with this candidate.
  1367→
  1368→    Checks for actual user message bubbles (not system prompts/tips) in the
  1369→    conversation area.
  1370→
  1371→    Args:
  1372→        page: Playwright page instance
  1373→        maimai_id: Candidate's Maimai ID
  1374→
  1375→    Returns:
  1376→        True if an existing conversation was detected
  1377→    """
  1378→    try:
  1379→        has_conversation = await page.evaluate('''() => {
  1380→            // Look for conversation message containers
  1381→            const chatSelectors = [
  1382→                '[class*="message-list"]',
  1383→                '[class*="chat-content"]',
  1384→                '[class*="conversation"]',
  1385→                '[class*="msg-list"]',
  1386→                '[class*="im-message"]',
  1387→                '[class*="chat-msg"]',
  1388→                '[class*="chat-record"]',
  1389→            ];
  1390→
  1391→            // System/tip patterns that should NOT count as existing conversation
  1392→            const systemPatterns = [
  1393→                '还不是好友', '添加好友', '发送好友申请', '对方未回复',
  1394→                '以上是打招呼内容', '暂无消息', '开始聊天', '你们已经是好友',
  1395→                '系统消息', '温馨提示', '安全提示', '注意事项',
  1396→                '开启招呼语', '求职意向', '目前情况', '竞业点',
  1397→                '欢迎联系', '不感兴趣',
  1398→            ];
  1399→
  1400→            const isSystemText = (text) => {
  1401→                const t = text.trim();
  1402→                if (t.length < 5) return true;  // Too short to be a real message
  1403→                return systemPatterns.some(p => t.includes(p));
  1404→            };
  1405→
  1406→            for (const sel of chatSelectors) {
  1407→                const container = document.querySelector(sel);
  1408→                if (!container) continue;
  1409→
  1410→                // Strategy 1: Look for message bubble elements
  1411→                const bubbleSelectors = [
  1412→                    '[class*="msg-item"]', '[class*="message-item"]',
  1413→                    '[class*="bubble"]', '[class*="chat-item"]',
  1414→                    '[class*="msg-bubble"]', '[class*="chat-bubble"]',
  1415→                ];
  1416→                for (const bSel of bubbleSelectors) {
  1417→                    const bubbles = container.querySelectorAll(bSel);
  1418→                    // Filter out system/tip bubbles
  1419→                    let realMessageCount = 0;
  1420→                    for (const bubble of bubbles) {
  1421→                        const text = bubble.textContent.trim();
  1422→                        if (text.length > 5 && !isSystemText(text)) {
  1423→                            realMessageCount++;
  1424→                        }
  1425→                    }
  1426→                    if (realMessageCount > 0) {
  1427→                        return true;
  1428→                    }
  1429→                }
  1430→
  1431→                // Strategy 2: If no bubble selectors matched, check direct children
  1432→                // that look like message blocks (not system tips)
  1433→                const children = container.children;
  1434→                let realChildCount = 0;
  1435→                for (const child of children) {
  1436→                    const text = child.textContent.trim();
  1437→                    // Must have meaningful content and not be a system message
  1438→                    if (text.length > 10 && !isSystemText(text)) {
  1439→                        // Check it's not a timestamp-only or status-only element
  1440→                        if (child.querySelector('img[class*="avatar"]') || text.length > 20) {
  1441→                            realChildCount++;
  1442→                        }
  1443→                    }
  1444→                }
  1445→                if (realChildCount > 0) {
  1446→                    return true;
  1447→                }
  1448→            }
  1449→            return false;
  1450→        }''')
  1451→
  1452→        if has_conversation:
  1453→            logger.info(f"Existing conversation detected for {maimai_id}")
  1454→        return has_conversation
  1455→
  1456→    except Exception as e:
  1457→        # On error, assume conversation exists (safe side: avoid duplicate messages)
  1458→        logger.warning(f"Conversation check failed for {maimai_id}: {e} — assuming existing conversation")
  1459→        return True
  1460→
  1461→
  1462→def _get_candidate_info(db_manager, maimai_id: str) -> Optional[Dict]:
  1463→    """Get candidate info from database."""
  1464→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1465→        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(maimai_id=maimai_id).first()
  1466→        if candidate:
  1467→            return {
  1468→                'name': candidate.name,
  1469→                'title': candidate.title,
  1470→                'company': candidate.company,
  1471→            }
  1472→        return None
  1473→
  1474→
  1475→def _log_message(
  1476→    db_manager,
  1477→    maimai_id: str,
  1478→    template_name: str,
  1479→    content: str,
  1480→    status: str
  1481→) -> None:
  1482→    """Log message to database. Creates a minimal Candidate if needed for FK."""
  1483→    try:
  1484→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1485→            # Ensure candidate exists for FK constraint
  1486→            existing = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(maimai_id=maimai_id).first()
  1487→            if not existing:
  1488→                session.add(Candidate(maimai_id=maimai_id))
  1489→                session.flush()
  1490→
  1491→            message = Message(
  1492→                candidate_maimai_id=maimai_id,
  1493→                template_name=template_name,
  1494→                content=content,
  1495→                status=status,
  1496→                sent_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) if status == 'sent' else None
  1497→            )
  1498→            session.add(message)
  1499→            logger.debug(f"Logged message to {maimai_id}: {status}")
  1500→    except Exception as e:
  1501→        logger.warning(
  1502→            f"AUDIT FAILURE: Failed to log message to {maimai_id} (status={status}): {e}. "
  1503→            f"This candidate may be re-messaged on next run."
  1504→        )
  1505→        # For sent messages, audit failure is critical — re-raise to prevent
  1506→        # silent duplicate messaging on the next pipeline run.
  1507→        if status == 'sent':
  1508→            raise
  1509→
  1510→
  1511→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1512→# Enterprise inline messaging (Path B)
  1513→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1514→
  1515→async def _close_enterprise_popup(page: Page) -> None:
  1516→    """Close popup/modal that appears after sending a message in enterprise flow."""
  1517→    config = get_config()
  1518→
  1519→    # Try close popup image first
  1520→    close_img_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.close_popup_img")
  1521→    try:
  1522→        close_img = await page.wait_for_selector(close_img_sel, timeout=3000)
  1523→        if close_img:
  1524→            await close_img.click()
  1525→            await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
  1526→    except Exception:
  1527→        pass  # Popup may not exist; best-effort
  1528→
  1529→    # Try close modal button
  1530→    close_modal_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.close_modal")
  1531→    try:
  1532→        close_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(close_modal_sel, timeout=2000)
  1533→        if close_btn:
  1534→            await close_btn.click()
  1535→            await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
  1536→    except Exception:
  1537→        pass  # Modal may not exist; best-effort
  1538→
  1539→
  1540→async def send_message_enterprise_inline(
  1541→    page: Page,
  1542→    content: str,
  1543→    config=None,
  1544→) -> bool:
  1545→    """Send a message inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1546→
  1547→    This operates on the current visible candidate card — clicks "立即沟通",
  1548→    types the message in the textarea, clicks "发送后留在此页", and closes
  1549→    the popup.
  1550→
  1551→    Args:
  1552→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1553→        content: Message content to send
  1554→        config: Config instance (loaded if None)
  1555→
  1556→    Returns:
  1557→        True if message was sent successfully
  1558→    """
  1559→    if config is None:
  1560→        config = get_config()
  1561→
  1562→    try:
  1563→        # Click 立即沟通
  1564→        chat_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.chat_now_button")
  1565→        chat_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(chat_sel, timeout=5000)
  1566→        if not chat_btn:
  1567→            logger.warning("Enterprise: 立即沟通 button not found")
  1568→            return False
  1569→
  1570→        await human_click(page, chat_sel)
  1571→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1572→
  1573→        # Wait for message textarea
  1574→        textarea_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.message_textarea")
  1575→        textarea = await page.wait_for_selector(textarea_sel, timeout=5000)
  1576→        if not textarea:
  1577→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message textarea not found")
  1578→            return False
  1579→
  1580→        # Clear any default text and type message
  1581→        await textarea.click()
  1582→        select_all_key = "Meta+a" if sys.platform == "darwin" else "Control+a"
  1583→        await page.keyboard.press(select_all_key)
  1584→        await page.keyboard.press("Backspace")
  1585→        await human_type(page, textarea_sel, content)
  1586→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1587→
  1588→        # Click 发送后留在此页
  1589→        send_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.send_stay_button")
  1590→        send_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(send_sel, timeout=5000)
  1591→        if not send_btn:
  1592→            logger.warning("Enterprise: send button not found")
  1593→            return False
  1594→
  1595→        await human_click(page, send_sel)
  1596→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1597→
  1598→        # Close popup/modal
  1599→        await _close_enterprise_popup(page)
  1600→
  1601→        # Verify message was actually sent
  1602→        verified = await _verify_message_sent(page, content)
  1603→        if not verified:
  1604→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message send could not be verified")
  1605→            return False
  1606→
  1607→        logger.info("Enterprise: message sent and verified inline")
  1608→        return True
  1609→
  1610→    except Exception as e:
  1611→        logger.error(f"Enterprise inline send failed: {e}")
  1612→        return False
  1613→
  1614→
  1615→async def send_message_batch_enterprise(
  1616→    page: Page,
  1617→    candidates: List[dict],
  1618→    template_name: str,
  1619→    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
  1620→    browser: 'BrowserManager',
  1621→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
  1622→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
  1623→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  1624→    """Batch send messages inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1625→
  1626→    Iterates visible candidate cards on the enterprise results page,
  1627→    sending messages inline without navigating to separate chat pages.
  1628→
  1629→    Args:
  1630→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1631→        candidates: List of candidate dicts with maimai_id, name, company, title
  1632→        template_name: Template name to use
  1633→        common_variables: Shared template variables
  1634→        browser: Browser manager instance
  1635→        skip_messaged: Skip already-messaged candidates
  1636→
  1637→    Returns:
  1638→        Dict with total, sent, failed, skipped, rate_limited counts
  1639→    """
  1640→    config = get_config()
  1641→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
  1642→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
  1643→
  1644→    try:
  1645→        template = config.get_template(template_name)
  1646→    except Exception:
  1647→        raise ValidationError(f"Template not found: {template_name}")
  1648→
  1649→    results = {
  1650→        "total": len(candidates),
  1651→        "sent": 0,
  1652→        "failed": 0,
  1653→        "skipped": 0,
  1654→        "rate_limited": 0,
  1655→        "details": [],
  1656→    }
  1657→
  1658→    # Filter already-messaged
  1659→    send_list = []
  1660→    if skip_messaged:
  1661→        # Batch pre-fetch all sent message IDs to avoid N+1 queries
  1662→        all_ids = [c["maimai_id"] for c in candidates]
  1663→        messaged_ids = set()
  1664→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1665→            rows = session.query(Message.candidate_maimai_id).filter(
  1666→                Message.candidate_maimai_id.in_(all_ids),
  1667→                Message.status == "sent"
  1668→            ).distinct().all()
  1669→            messaged_ids = {row[0] for row in rows}
  1670→
  1671→        for c in candidates:
  1672→            if c["maimai_id"] in messaged_ids:
  1673→                results["skipped"] += 1
  1674→                results["details"].append({
  1675→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1676→                    "name": c["name"],
  1677→                    "status": "skipped",
  1678→                    "reason": "already messaged",
  1679→                })
  1680→            else:
  1681→                send_list.append(c)
  1682→    else:
  1683→        send_list = list(candidates)
  1684→
  1685→    if not send_list:
  1686→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates to send messages to.[/yellow]")
  1687→        return results
  1688→
  1689→    # Build preview
  1690→    first = send_list[0]
  1691→    first_vars = {
  1692→        **common_variables,
  1693→        "name": first["name"],
  1694→        "company": first["company"],
  1695→        "position": first["title"],
  1696→    }
  1697→    first_content = render_template(template["content"], first_vars)
  1698→
  1699→    # Display summary
  1700→    summary_text = Text()
  1701→    summary_text.append("Template: ", style="bold")
  1702→    summary_text.append(f"{template_name}\n")
  1703→    summary_text.append("Mode: ", style="bold")
  1704→    summary_text.append("Enterprise Inline\n")
  1705→    summary_text.append("Candidates: ", style="bold")
  1706→    summary_text.append(f"{len(candidates)} found, {results['skipped']} already messaged\n")
  1707→    summary_text.append("Will send to: ", style="bold")
  1708→    summary_text.append(f"{len(send_list)} candidates\n\n")
  1709→    summary_text.append("Message Preview ", style="bold cyan")
  1710→    summary_text.append(f"(first candidate):\n", style="dim")
  1711→    summary_text.append("To: ", style="bold cyan")
  1712→    recipient = first["name"]
  1713→    if first.get("title") and first.get("company"):
  1714→        recipient += f" ({first['title']} @ {first['company']})"
  1715→    summary_text.append(f"{recipient}\n\n")
  1716→    summary_text.append(first_content)
  1717→
  1718→    console.print(Panel(
  1719→        summary_text,
  1720→        title="Enterprise Pipeline Summary",
  1721→        border_style="green",
  1722→        padding=(1, 2),
  1723→    ))
  1724→
  1725→    # Confirmation
  1726→    if skip_confirmation:
  1727→        console.print(f"[green]Auto-confirmed: sending to {len(send_list)} candidates (enterprise)[/green]")
  1728→    else:
  1729→        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
  1730→        confirm = await loop.run_in_executor(
  1731→            None,
  1732→            lambda: questionary.confirm(
  1733→                f"Confirm batch send to {len(send_list)} candidates?"
  1734→            ).unsafe_ask(),
  1735→        )
  1736→
  1737→        if not confirm:
  1738→            console.print("[yellow]Batch send cancelled.[/yellow]")
  1739→            return results
  1740→
  1741→    # Send messages
  1742→    from rich.progress import Progress, BarColumn, TextColumn as ProgressTextColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
  1743→
  1744→    with Progress(
  1745→        ProgressTextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
  1746→        BarColumn(),
  1747→        MofNCompleteColumn(),
  1748→        console=console,
  1749→    ) as progress:
  1750→        task = progress.add_task("Sending messages (enterprise)...", total=len(send_list))
  1751→
  1752→        for c in send_list:
  1753→            # Rate limit check
  1754→            try:
  1755→                await async_check_rate_limit("send_message")
  1756→            except RateLimitExceededError as e:
  1757→                console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
  1758→                remaining = len(send_list) - (results["sent"] + results["failed"])
  1759→                results["rate_limited"] = remaining
  1760→                results["details"].append({
  1761→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1762→                    "name": c["name"],
  1763→                    "status": "rate_limited",
  1764→                    "reason": str(e),
  1765→                })
  1766→                break
  1767→
  1768→            # Build per-candidate variables
  1769→            variables = {
  1770→                **common_variables,
  1771→                "name": c["name"],
  1772→                "company": c["company"],
  1773→                "position": c["title"],
  1774→            }
  1775→            content = render_template(template["content"], variables)
  1776→
  1777→            # Scroll to find the candidate card (by name match)
  1778→            try:
  1779→                card_locator = page.get_by_text(c["name"], exact=False).first
  1780→                await card_locator.scroll_into_view_if_needed(timeout=5000)
  1781→                await async_random_delay("action")
  1782→            except Exception as e:
  1783→                logger.warning(f"Could not scroll to card for '{c['name']}': {e}")
  1784→
  1785→            # Send inline
  1786→            success = await send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config)
  1787→
  1788→            if success:
  1789→                results["sent"] += 1
  1790→                results["details"].append({
  1791→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1792→                    "name": c["name"],
  1793→                    "status": "sent",
  1794→                })
  1795→                rate_limiter.log_action("send_message", {
  1796→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1797→                    "template": template_name,
  1798→                    "mode": "enterprise",
  1799→                })
  1800→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "sent")
  1801→            else:
  1802→                results["failed"] += 1
  1803→                results["details"].append({
  1804→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1805→                    "name": c["name"],
  1806→                    "status": "failed",
  1807→                    "reason": "inline send returned False",
  1808→                })
  1809→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "failed")
  1810→
  1811→            progress.advance(task)
  1812→            await async_random_delay("message")
  1813→
  1814→    return results
  1815→

"""Lightweight database migration system.

Tracks schema versions in a dedicated table and applies incremental
migrations on startup.  Each migration is a plain function that
receives a SQLAlchemy connection.
"""

from typing import Callable, List, Tuple

from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
from loguru import logger


# Migration registry: list of (version, description, migration_fn)
# Append new migrations at the end; never reorder or remove existing entries.
_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []


def migration(version: int, description: str):
    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    def decorator(fn: Callable):
        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
        return fn
    return decorator


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Built-in migrations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    """Ensure core tables exist (safe for existing databases)."""
    # SQLAlchemy create_all is called before migrations, so tables should
    # already exist.  This migration just marks the baseline version.
    pass


@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    inspector = inspect(conn)
    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    if "retry_count" not in columns:
        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Migration runner
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    """Create the schema_version table if it doesn't exist."""
    conn.execute(text("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            description TEXT NOT NULL,
            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        )
    """))
    conn.commit()


def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    """Return highest applied migration version, or 0 if none."""
    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    row = result.fetchone()
    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0


def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    """Apply all pending migrations against *engine*.

    Called automatically by DatabaseManager.init_db().
    """
    with engine.connect() as conn:
        _ensure_version_table(conn)
        current = _current_version(conn)

        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
        if not pending:
            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
            return

        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
            fn(conn)
            conn.execute(
                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
                {"v": version, "d": description},
            )
            conn.commit()

        final = _current_version(conn)
        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")

1→"""Search module for finding candidates on Maimai.
     2→
     3→Handles candidate search with filters, pagination,
     4→and extraction of basic candidate information.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from dataclasses import dataclass
     8→from typing import List, Optional
     9→from urllib.parse import urlencode
    10→
    11→from patchright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.progress import Progress, SpinnerColumn, TextColumn
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.core.anti_detection import human_scroll, human_type
    18→from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    19→from src.utils.config import get_config
    20→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    21→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    22→from src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    23→from src.utils.validators import validate_keywords
    24→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    25→from src.models.database import Candidate, SearchHistory, get_db_manager
    26→from src.exceptions.errors import (
    27→    BrowserError,
    28→    SessionExpiredError,
    29→    SelectorNotFoundError,
    30→)
    31→
    32→console = Console()
    33→
    34→
    35→# Maimai search URL
    36→MAIMAI_SEARCH_URL = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
    37→
    38→# Names indicating paid/premium-only profiles that should be skipped
    39→PREMIUM_PLACEHOLDER_NAMES = {"脉脉高价值人脉"}
    40→
    41→# Keywords indicating non-R&D roles (to be filtered out)
    42→NON_RD_KEYWORDS = {
    43→    "销售", "运营", "产品", "市场", "电子商务", "电商",
    44→    "商务", "品牌", "公关", "客服", "采购", "招商",
    45→    "人力", "人事", "HR", "hr", "HRBP", "行政",
    46→    "财务", "会计", "审计", "法务", "法律", "合规",
    47→    "内容", "编辑", "翻译", "教育", "培训", "教学",
    48→    "物流", "供应链", "仓储",
    49→}
    50→
    51→

    28→    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    29→        """Load JSON configuration file."""
    30→        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    31→        try:
    32→            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    33→                return json.load(f)
    34→        except FileNotFoundError:
    35→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    36→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    37→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")

    45→        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")

    47→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    48→        """Get setting by dot-notation key (e.g., 'browser.headless').
    49→
    50→        Returns the configured value, including explicit ``null`` (None).
    51→        Only falls back to *default* when the key path does not exist.
    52→        """
    53→        keys = key.split('.')
    54→        value = self._settings
    55→
    56→        for k in keys:
    57→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    58→                value = value.get(k, _MISSING)
    59→                if value is _MISSING:
    60→                    return default
    61→            else:
    62→                return default
    63→
    64→        return value

    66→    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:

    69→        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    70→
    71→        for k in keys:

    76→            else:
    77→                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    78→
    79→        return value

"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""

import asyncio
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Tuple, Optional

from loguru import logger
from sqlalchemy import func

from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError


class RateLimiter:
    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.

    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.

        Args:
            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
        """
        self.config = config or get_config()
        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()

        # Adaptive throttle state
        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
        self._pruned: bool = False  # one-time prune flag

    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
        self._update_throttle_factor()

    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha frequency.

        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
        """
        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)

        if captcha_count >= 3:
            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
        elif captcha_count >= 2:
            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
        elif captcha_count >= 1:

"""Authentication module for Maimai login.

In CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
CLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
if the user is not logged in.
"""

import asyncio
from typing import Optional

from patchright.async_api import Page
from loguru import logger
from rich.console import Console
from rich.panel import Panel

from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError

console = Console()


async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    """
    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.

    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.

    Args:
        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)

    Returns:
        True if logged in
    """
    if browser is None:
        browser = get_browser_manager()

    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    page = await browser.start()

    # Check if already logged in
    if await check_login_status(page):
        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
        # Save session as backup
        await browser.save_session()
        return True

    # Not logged in — guide the user
    console.print(Panel(

input.content: """Web service layer for Maimai Assistant."""
"""API routers for the web service."""

"""Serial browser task queue.

All browser operations go through this queue to ensure only one
browser action runs at a time (Chrome CDP is a serial resource).
Non-browser operations (DB queries, template listing) bypass the queue.
"""

import asyncio
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import IntEnum
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, Optional

from loguru import logger


class TaskPriority(IntEnum):
    """Task priority levels. Lower value = higher priority."""
    HIGH = 1      # Status checks
    NORMAL = 5    # Search, message
    LOW = 10      # Pipeline (long-running)


class TaskStatus:
    QUEUED = "queued"
    RUNNING = "running"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    FAILED = "failed"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"
    AWAITING_CONFIRMATION = "awaiting_confirmation"


class BrowserTask:
    """A task to be executed in the browser queue."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        coroutine_factory: Callable[[], Awaitable[Any]],
        priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL,
        task_id: Optional[str] = None,
        user: str = "anonymous",
    ):
        self.task_id = task_id or str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
        self.name = name
        self.coroutine_factory = coroutine_factory
        self.priority = priority
        self.user = user
        self.status = TaskStatus.QUEUED
        self.result: Any = None
        self.error: Optional[str] = None
        self.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self.started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
        self.completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
        # asyncio.Event for callers that want to wait for completion
        self._done_event = asyncio.Event()
        # asyncio.Event for pipeline confirmation flow
        self.confirmation_event: Optional[asyncio.Event] = None
        self.confirmation_data: Optional[Dict] = None

    def __lt__(self, other: 'BrowserTask') -> bool:
        """Compare by (priority, creation time) for PriorityQueue."""
        if self.priority != other.priority:
            return self.priority < other.priority
        return self.created_at < other.created_at

    async def wait(self, timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        """Wait for task completion."""
        if timeout:
            await asyncio.wait_for(self._done_event.wait(), timeout=timeout)
        else:
            await self._done_event.wait()

    def mark_done(self) -> None:
        self._done_event.set()

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "task_id": self.task_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "status": self.status,
            "priority": self.priority.name,
            "user": self.user,
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
            "started_at": self.started_at.isoformat() if self.started_at else None,
            "completed_at": self.completed_at.isoformat() if self.completed_at else None,
            "error": self.error,
        }


class TaskQueue:
    """Async serial task queue for browser operations.

    Uses asyncio.PriorityQueue so higher-priority tasks run first.
    A single background worker consumes tasks one at a time.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._queue: asyncio.PriorityQueue[BrowserTask] = asyncio.PriorityQueue()
        self._worker_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
        self._tasks: Dict[str, BrowserTask] = {}  # task_id -> task
        self._running: bool = False
        self._current_task: Optional[BrowserTask] = None

    async def start(self) -> None:
        """Start the background worker."""
        if self._running:
            return
        self._running = True
        self._worker_task = asyncio.create_task(self._worker())
        logger.info("Task queue worker started")

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop the background worker."""
        self._running = False
        if self._worker_task:
            self._worker_task.cancel()
            try:
                await self._worker_task
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                pass
        logger.info("Task queue worker stopped")

    async def submit(self, task: BrowserTask) -> BrowserTask:
        """Submit a task to the queue. Returns the task for tracking."""
        self._tasks[task.task_id] = task
        await self._queue.put(task)
        logger.info(f"Task queued: {task.task_id} ({task.name}) priority={task.priority.name}")
        return task

    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[BrowserTask]:
        """Get a task by ID."""
        return self._tasks.get(task_id)

    @property
    def current_task(self) -> Optional[BrowserTask]:
        return self._current_task

    @property
    def queue_size(self) -> int:
        return self._queue.qsize()

    def get_queue_status(self) -> dict:
        """Get current queue status."""
        pending = [
            t.to_dict() for t in self._tasks.values()
            if t.status == TaskStatus.QUEUED
        ]
        return {
            "queue_size": self._queue.qsize(),
            "current_task": self._current_task.to_dict() if self._current_task else None,
            "pending_tasks": pending,
            "total_tracked": len(self._tasks),
        }

    def get_recent_tasks(self, limit: int = 20) -> list:
        """Get recent tasks sorted by creation time (newest first)."""
        tasks = sorted(
            self._tasks.values(),
            key=lambda t: t.created_at,
            reverse=True,
        )
        return [t.to_dict() for t in tasks[:limit]]

    async def _worker(self) -> None:
        """Background worker: dequeue and execute tasks serially."""
        while self._running:
            try:
                task = await asyncio.wait_for(self._queue.get(), timeout=1.0)
            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
                continue
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                break

            self._current_task = task
            task.status = TaskStatus.RUNNING
            task.started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            logger.info(f"Task running: {task.task_id} ({task.name})")

            try:
                task.result = await task.coroutine_factory()
                task.status = TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                logger.info(f"Task completed: {task.task_id}")
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                task.status = TaskStatus.CANCELLED
                task.error = "cancelled"
                logger.warning(f"Task cancelled: {task.task_id}")
            except Exception as e:
                task.status = TaskStatus.FAILED
                task.error = str(e)
                logger.error(f"Task failed: {task.task_id} — {e}")
            finally:
                task.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
                task.mark_done()
                self._current_task = None
                self._queue.task_done()

        logger.info("Task queue worker exiting")

    def cleanup_old_tasks(self, max_age_hours: int = 24) -> int:
        """Remove completed/failed tasks older than max_age_hours."""
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - __import__('datetime').timedelta(hours=max_age_hours)
        to_remove = [
            tid for tid, t in self._tasks.items()
            if t.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.FAILED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED)
            and t.completed_at and t.completed_at < cutoff
        ]
        for tid in to_remove:
            del self._tasks[tid]
        return len(to_remove)

"""WebSocket progress broadcaster and contextvars-based callback injection.

This module provides two things:
1. A Broadcaster that manages WebSocket connections and pushes events
2. A contextvars-based emit_progress() function that existing modules can call
   without knowing about WebSocket — in CLI mode, the context var is None
   and calls are silently ignored.
"""

import asyncio
import contextvars
import json
from typing import Any, Callable, Coroutine, Dict, Optional, Set

from fastapi import WebSocket
from loguru import logger


# Context variable for injecting progress callback into existing modules.
# In CLI mode this is None; in Web mode it's set before calling pipeline/message.
_progress_ctx: contextvars.ContextVar[Optional[Callable]] = contextvars.ContextVar(
    '_progress_ctx', default=None
)


def set_progress_callback(callback: Optional[Callable]) -> contextvars.Token:
    """Set the progress callback for the current async context."""
    return _progress_ctx.set(callback)


def emit_progress(event: str, **data: Any) -> None:
    """Emit a progress event if a callback is registered.

    Safe to call from anywhere — does nothing in CLI mode.
    In web mode, schedules the async callback as a fire-and-forget task.
    """
    cb = _progress_ctx.get()
    if cb is not None:
        try:
            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
            loop.create_task(cb(event, data))
        except RuntimeError:
            pass  # No running event loop (shouldn't happen in web mode)


class Broadcaster:
    """Manages WebSocket connections and broadcasts task events.

    Supports two types of subscriptions:
    - Task-specific: /ws/task/{task_id} — receives events for one task
    - Global: /ws/global — receives all task events
    """

    def __init__(self):
        # task_id -> set of WebSocket connections
        self._task_subs: Dict[str, Set[WebSocket]] = {}
        # Global subscribers
        self._global_subs: Set[WebSocket] = set()

    async def subscribe_task(self, task_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
        """Subscribe a WebSocket to events for a specific task."""
        if task_id not in self._task_subs:
            self._task_subs[task_id] = set()
        self._task_subs[task_id].add(ws)

    async def unsubscribe_task(self, task_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
        """Unsubscribe a WebSocket from a task."""
        if task_id in self._task_subs:
            self._task_subs[task_id].discard(ws)
            if not self._task_subs[task_id]:
                del self._task_subs[task_id]

    async def subscribe_global(self, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
        self._global_subs.add(ws)

    async def unsubscribe_global(self, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
        self._global_subs.discard(ws)

    async def broadcast(self, task_id: str, event: str, data: dict) -> None:
        """Broadcast an event to task subscribers and global subscribers."""
        message = json.dumps({
            "task_id": task_id,
            "event": event,
            **data,
        })

        # Send to task-specific subscribers
        dead: list = []
        for ws in self._task_subs.get(task_id, set()):
            try:
                await ws.send_text(message)
            except Exception:
                dead.append(ws)
        for ws in dead:
            self._task_subs.get(task_id, set()).discard(ws)

        # Send to global subscribers
        dead = []
        for ws in self._global_subs:
            try:
                await ws.send_text(message)
            except Exception:
                dead.append(ws)
        for ws in dead:
            self._global_subs.discard(ws)

    def make_progress_callback(self, task_id: str) -> Callable:
        """Create a progress callback bound to a specific task_id.

        Returns an async callable suitable for set_progress_callback().
        """
        async def callback(event: str, data: dict) -> None:
            await self.broadcast(task_id, event, data)

        return callback

"""Dependency injection for FastAPI endpoints.

_task_queue: TaskQueue | None = None
_broadcaster: Broadcaster | None = None

    global _task_queue, _broadcaster
    _task_queue = queue
    _broadcaster = broadcaster


def get_queue() -> TaskQueue:
    assert _task_queue is not None, "TaskQueue not initialized"
    return _task_queue


def get_broadcaster() -> Broadcaster:

    return _broadcaster


def get_browser() -> BrowserManager:
    return get_browser_manager()


def get_db() -> DatabaseManager:
    return get_db_manager()

"""Serial browser task queue.

All browser operations go through this queue to ensure only one
browser action runs at a time (Chrome CDP is a serial resource).
Non-browser operations (DB queries, template listing) bypass the queue.
"""

import asyncio
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import IntEnum
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, Optional

from loguru import logger


class TaskPriority(IntEnum):
    """Task priority levels. Lower value = higher priority."""
    HIGH = 1      # Status checks
    NORMAL = 5    # Search, message
    LOW = 10      # Pipeline (long-running)


class TaskStatus:
    QUEUED = "queued"
    RUNNING = "running"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    FAILED = "failed"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"
    AWAITING_CONFIRMATION = "awaiting_confirmation"


class BrowserTask:
    """A task to be executed in the browser queue."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        coroutine_factory: Callable[[], Awaitable[Any]],
        priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL,
        task_id: Optional[str] = None,
        user: str = "anonymous",
    ):
        self.task_id = task_id or str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
        self.name = name
        self.coroutine_factory = coroutine_factory
        self.priority = priority
        self.user = user
        self.status = TaskStatus.QUEUED
        self.result: Any = None
        self.error: Optional[str] = None
        self.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self.started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
        self.completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
        # asyncio.Event for callers that want to wait for completion
        self._done_event = asyncio.Event()
        # asyncio.Event for pipeline confirmation flow
        self.confirmation_event: Optional[asyncio.Event] = None
        self.confirmation_data: Optional[Dict] = None

    def __lt__(self, other: 'BrowserTask') -> bool:
        """Compare by (priority, creation time) for PriorityQueue."""
        if self.priority != other.priority:
            return self.priority < other.priority
        return self.created_at < other.created_at

    async def wait(self, timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        """Wait for task completion."""
        if timeout:
            await asyncio.wait_for(self._done_event.wait(), timeout=timeout)
        else:
            await self._done_event.wait()

    def mark_done(self) -> None:
        self._done_event.set()

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "task_id": self.task_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "status": self.status,
            "priority": self.priority.name,
            "user": self.user,
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
            "started_at": self.started_at.isoformat() if self.started_at else None,
            "completed_at": self.completed_at.isoformat() if self.completed_at else None,
            "error": self.error,
        }


class TaskQueue:
    """Async serial task queue for browser operations.

    Uses asyncio.PriorityQueue so higher-priority tasks run first.
    A single background worker consumes tasks one at a time.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._queue: asyncio.PriorityQueue[BrowserTask] = asyncio.PriorityQueue()
        self._worker_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
        self._tasks: Dict[str, BrowserTask] = {}  # task_id -> task
        self._running: bool = False
        self._current_task: Optional[BrowserTask] = None

    async def start(self) -> None:
        """Start the background worker."""
        if self._running:
            return
        self._running = True
        self._worker_task = asyncio.create_task(self._worker())
        logger.info("Task queue worker started")

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop the background worker."""
        self._running = False
        if self._worker_task:
            self._worker_task.cancel()
            try:
                await self._worker_task
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                pass
        logger.info("Task queue worker stopped")

    async def submit(self, task: BrowserTask) -> BrowserTask:
        """Submit a task to the queue. Returns the task for tracking."""
        self._tasks[task.task_id] = task
        await self._queue.put(task)
        logger.info(f"Task queued: {task.task_id} ({task.name}) priority={task.priority.name}")
        return task

    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[BrowserTask]:
        """Get a task by ID."""
        return self._tasks.get(task_id)

    @property
    def current_task(self) -> Optional[BrowserTask]:
        return self._current_task

    @property
    def queue_size(self) -> int:
        return self._queue.qsize()

    def get_queue_status(self) -> dict:
        """Get current queue status."""
        pending = [
            t.to_dict() for t in self._tasks.values()
            if t.status == TaskStatus.QUEUED
        ]
        return {
            "queue_size": self._queue.qsize(),
            "current_task": self._current_task.to_dict() if self._current_task else None,
            "pending_tasks": pending,
            "total_tracked": len(self._tasks),
        }

    def get_recent_tasks(self, limit: int = 20) -> list:
        """Get recent tasks sorted by creation time (newest first)."""
        tasks = sorted(
            self._tasks.values(),
            key=lambda t: t.created_at,
            reverse=True,
        )
        return [t.to_dict() for t in tasks[:limit]]

    async def _worker(self) -> None:
        """Background worker: dequeue and execute tasks serially."""
        while self._running:
            try:
                task = await asyncio.wait_for(self._queue.get(), timeout=1.0)
            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
                continue
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                break

            self._current_task = task
            task.status = TaskStatus.RUNNING
            task.started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            logger.info(f"Task running: {task.task_id} ({task.name})")

            try:
                task.result = await task.coroutine_factory()
                task.status = TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                logger.info(f"Task completed: {task.task_id}")
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                task.status = TaskStatus.CANCELLED
                task.error = "cancelled"
                logger.warning(f"Task cancelled: {task.task_id}")
            except Exception as e:
                task.status = TaskStatus.FAILED
                task.error = str(e)
                logger.error(f"Task failed: {task.task_id} — {e}")
            finally:
                task.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
                task.mark_done()
                self._current_task = None
                self._queue.task_done()

        logger.info("Task queue worker exiting")

    def cleanup_old_tasks(self, max_age_hours: int = 24) -> int:
        """Remove completed/failed tasks older than max_age_hours."""
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - __import__('datetime').timedelta(hours=max_age_hours)
        to_remove = [
            tid for tid, t in self._tasks.items()
            if t.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.FAILED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED)
            and t.completed_at and t.completed_at < cutoff
        ]
        for tid in to_remove:
            del self._tasks[tid]
        return len(to_remove)

class Broadcaster:
    """Manages WebSocket connections and broadcasts task events.

    Supports two types of subscriptions:
    - Task-specific: /ws/task/{task_id} — receives events for one task
    - Global: /ws/global — receives all task events
    """

        # task_id -> set of WebSocket connections
        self._task_subs: Dict[str, Set[WebSocket]] = {}
        # Global subscribers
        self._global_subs: Set[WebSocket] = set()

    async def subscribe_task(self, task_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:

        if task_id not in self._task_subs:
            self._task_subs[task_id] = set()
        self._task_subs[task_id].add(ws)

                del self._task_subs[task_id]

    async def subscribe_global(self, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
        self._global_subs.add(ws)

    async def unsubscribe_global(self, ws: WebSocket) -> None:

    async def broadcast(self, task_id: str, event: str, data: dict) -> None:

"""Dependency injection for FastAPI endpoints.

Provides shared singleton instances of BrowserManager, DatabaseManager,
TaskQueue, and Broadcaster via FastAPI's Depends() system.
"""

from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
from src.models.database import DatabaseManager, get_db_manager
from src.web.task_queue import TaskQueue
from src.web.progress import Broadcaster


# Module-level singletons (initialized in app lifespan)
_task_queue: TaskQueue | None = None
_broadcaster: Broadcaster | None = None


def init_singletons(queue: TaskQueue, broadcaster: Broadcaster) -> None:
    """Called once during app lifespan startup."""
    global _task_queue, _broadcaster
    _task_queue = queue
    _broadcaster = broadcaster


def get_queue() -> TaskQueue:
    assert _task_queue is not None, "TaskQueue not initialized"
    return _task_queue


def get_broadcaster() -> Broadcaster:
    assert _broadcaster is not None, "Broadcaster not initialized"
    return _broadcaster


def get_browser() -> BrowserManager:
    return get_browser_manager()


def get_db() -> DatabaseManager:
    return get_db_manager()

"""FastAPI application entry point.

Usage:
    python -m src.web.app [--host 0.0.0.0] [--port 8000] [--cdp http://localhost:9222]
"""

import argparse
import sys
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates
from loguru import logger

from src.web.task_queue import TaskQueue
from src.web.progress import Broadcaster
from src.web.deps import init_singletons


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    """Startup / shutdown lifecycle."""
    # Initialize singletons
    queue = TaskQueue()
    broadcaster = Broadcaster()
    init_singletons(queue, broadcaster)

    # Store on app.state for templates/ws access
    app.state.queue = queue
    app.state.broadcaster = broadcaster

    # Start task queue worker
    await queue.start()

    # Initialize database (triggers WAL mode + migrations)
    from src.models.database import get_db_manager
    get_db_manager()

    logger.info("Web service started")
    yield

    # Shutdown
    await queue.stop()
    logger.info("Web service stopped")


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    """Create and configure the FastAPI application."""
    app = FastAPI(
        title="Maimai Assistant",
        description="Semi-automated recruitment assistant for Maimai",
        version="1.0.0",
        lifespan=lifespan,
    )

    # --- Static files & templates ---
    project_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent
    static_dir = project_root / "static"
    templates_dir = project_root / "templates"
    static_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    templates_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=str(static_dir)), name="static")

    # Store templates on app state for routers to access
    app.state.templates = Jinja2Templates(directory=str(templates_dir))

    # --- Register API routers ---
    from src.web.routers import auth, search, candidates, message, pipeline, templates, status, export, ws

    app.include_router(auth.router, prefix="/api/auth", tags=["auth"])
    app.include_router(search.router, prefix="/api", tags=["search"])
    app.include_router(candidates.router, prefix="/api", tags=["candidates"])
    app.include_router(message.router, prefix="/api", tags=["message"])
    app.include_router(pipeline.router, prefix="/api", tags=["pipeline"])
    app.include_router(templates.router, prefix="/api", tags=["templates"])
    app.include_router(status.router, prefix="/api", tags=["status"])
    app.include_router(export.router, prefix="/api", tags=["export"])
    app.include_router(ws.router, tags=["websocket"])

    # --- Page routes (Jinja2 HTML) ---
    from src.web.routers import pages
    app.include_router(pages.router, tags=["pages"])

    return app


# Module-level app instance for uvicorn
app = create_app()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Maimai Assistant Web Service")
    parser.add_argument("--host", default="0.0.0.0", help="Bind host")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8000, help="Bind port")
    parser.add_argument("--cdp", default=None, help="Chrome CDP endpoint")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.cdp:
        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
        get_browser_manager().set_cdp_endpoint(args.cdp)

    uvicorn.run(
        "src.web.app:app",
        host=args.host,
        port=args.port,
        reload=False,
        log_level="info",
    )

"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""

from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from sqlalchemy import (
    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
from loguru import logger

from src.utils.config import get_config

Base = declarative_base()


def _utcnow():
    """Return timezone-aware UTC now (replaces deprecated datetime.utcnow)."""
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


class Candidate(Base):
    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""

    __tablename__ = 'candidates'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    name = Column(String(100))
    title = Column(String(200))
    company = Column(String(200))
    location = Column(String(100))
    profile_url = Column(Text)

    # JSON fields for structured data
    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    education = Column(JSON)
    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    skills = Column(JSON)

    # Metadata
    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate

    # Relationships
    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"


class Message(Base):
    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""

    __tablename__ = 'messages'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    candidate_maimai_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.maimai_id'), index=True)
    template_name = Column(String(100))
    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)

    # Relationships
    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"


class SearchHistory(Base):
    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""

    __tablename__ = 'search_history'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON
    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"


class ActionLog(Base):
    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""

    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details

    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
    __table_args__ = (
        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"


class DatabaseManager:
    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
        """Initialize database manager."""
        if db_path is None:
            config = get_config()
            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')

        # Ensure data directory exists
        db_file = Path(db_path)
        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Create engine and session
        # StaticPool uses a single connection, avoiding SQLite "database is locked" errors
        self.db_path = db_path
        self.engine = create_engine(
            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
            echo=False,
            poolclass=StaticPool,
            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
        )
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
        self.init_db()

        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")

    def init_db(self):
        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")

        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
        run_migrations(self.engine)

    @contextmanager
    def session_scope(self):
        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
        session = self.Session()
        try:
            yield session
            session.commit()
        except Exception:
            session.rollback()
            raise
        finally:
            session.close()

    def drop_all(self):
        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")


# Global database manager instance
_db_manager = None


def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
    """Get global database manager instance."""
    global _db_manager
    if _db_manager is None:
        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
    return _db_manager

    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details

    __table_args__ = (

class DatabaseManager:
    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
        """Initialize database manager."""
        if db_path is None:
            config = get_config()
            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')

        # Ensure data directory exists
        db_file = Path(db_path)
        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Create engine and session
        # StaticPool uses a single connection, avoiding SQLite "database is locked" errors
        self.db_path = db_path
        self.engine = create_engine(
            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
            echo=False,
            poolclass=StaticPool,
            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
        )
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
        self.init_db()

        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")

    def init_db(self):
        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")

        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
        run_migrations(self.engine)

    @contextmanager
    def session_scope(self):
        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
        session = self.Session()
        try:
            yield session
            session.commit()
        except Exception:
            session.rollback()
            raise
        finally:
            session.close()

    def drop_all(self):
        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")


# Global database manager instance
_db_manager = None


def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
    """Get global database manager instance."""
    global _db_manager
    if _db_manager is None:
        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
    return _db_manager

def _set_wal_mode(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
    """Enable WAL mode on every new SQLite connection.

    WAL mode allows concurrent readers with a single writer,
    suitable for 2-5 concurrent web users.
    """
    cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    cursor.close()

            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')

        db_file = Path(db_path)
        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Use QueuePool with WAL mode for concurrent read support (web service).
        # check_same_thread=False is still needed for SQLite in multi-threaded context.
        self.db_path = db_path
        self.engine = create_engine(
            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
            echo=False,
            poolclass=QueuePool,
            pool_size=5,
            max_overflow=10,
            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},

        # Enable WAL mode on every connection
        event.listen(self.engine, "connect", _set_wal_mode)

"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""

from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from sqlalchemy import (
    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint, event
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.pool import QueuePool, StaticPool
from loguru import logger

from src.utils.config import get_config

Base = declarative_base()


def _utcnow():
    """Return timezone-aware UTC now (replaces deprecated datetime.utcnow)."""
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


class Candidate(Base):
    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""

    __tablename__ = 'candidates'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    name = Column(String(100))
    title = Column(String(200))
    company = Column(String(200))
    location = Column(String(100))
    profile_url = Column(Text)

    # JSON fields for structured data
    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    education = Column(JSON)
    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    skills = Column(JSON)

    # Metadata
    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate

    # Relationships
    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"


class Message(Base):
    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""

    __tablename__ = 'messages'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    candidate_maimai_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.maimai_id'), index=True)
    template_name = Column(String(100))
    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)

    # Relationships
    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"


class SearchHistory(Base):
    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""

    __tablename__ = 'search_history'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON
    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"


class ActionLog(Base):
    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""

    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details

    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
    __table_args__ = (
        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"


class DatabaseManager:
    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
        """Initialize database manager."""
        if db_path is None:
            config = get_config()
            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')

        # Ensure data directory exists
        db_file = Path(db_path)
        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Create engine and session
        # StaticPool uses a single connection, avoiding SQLite "database is locked" errors
        self.db_path = db_path
        self.engine = create_engine(
            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
            echo=False,
            poolclass=StaticPool,
            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
        )
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
        self.init_db()

        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")

    def init_db(self):
        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")

        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
        run_migrations(self.engine)

    @contextmanager
    def session_scope(self):
        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
        session = self.Session()
        try:
            yield session
            session.commit()
        except Exception:
            session.rollback()
            raise
        finally:
            session.close()

    def drop_all(self):
        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")


# Global database manager instance
_db_manager = None


def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
    """Get global database manager instance."""
    global _db_manager
    if _db_manager is None:
        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
    return _db_manager

"""Lightweight database migration system.

Tracks schema versions in a dedicated table and applies incremental
migrations on startup.  Each migration is a plain function that
receives a SQLAlchemy connection.
"""

from typing import Callable, List, Tuple

from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
from loguru import logger


# Migration registry: list of (version, description, migration_fn)
# Append new migrations at the end; never reorder or remove existing entries.
_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []


def migration(version: int, description: str):
    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    def decorator(fn: Callable):
        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
        return fn
    return decorator


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Built-in migrations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    """Ensure core tables exist (safe for existing databases)."""
    # SQLAlchemy create_all is called before migrations, so tables should
    # already exist.  This migration just marks the baseline version.
    pass


@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    inspector = inspect(conn)
    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    if "retry_count" not in columns:
        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Migration runner
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    """Create the schema_version table if it doesn't exist."""
    conn.execute(text("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            description TEXT NOT NULL,
            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        )
    """))
    conn.commit()


def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    """Return highest applied migration version, or 0 if none."""
    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    row = result.fetchone()
    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0


def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    """Apply all pending migrations against *engine*.

    Called automatically by DatabaseManager.init_db().
    """
    with engine.connect() as conn:
        _ensure_version_table(conn)
        current = _current_version(conn)

        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
        if not pending:
            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
            return

        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
            fn(conn)
            conn.execute(
                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
                {"v": version, "d": description},
            )
            conn.commit()

        final = _current_version(conn)
        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")

    conn.commit()


def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    """Return highest applied migration version, or 0 if none."""
    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    row = result.fetchone()
    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0


def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    """Apply all pending migrations against *engine*.

    Called automatically by DatabaseManager.init_db().
    """
    with engine.connect() as conn:
        _ensure_version_table(conn)
        current = _current_version(conn)

        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
        if not pending:
            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
            return

        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
            fn(conn)
            conn.execute(
                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
                {"v": version, "d": description},
            )
            conn.commit()

        final = _current_version(conn)
        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")
